1180	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
1181	```
1182	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
1183	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
1184	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
1185	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
1186	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
1187	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
1188	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
1189	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
1190	
1191	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/
1192	
1193	/-! ### Lane G iter-195 structural carving: inductive-step helper.
1194	
1195	The `pd_R(M) = k + 1` inductive step of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula is
1196	extracted into the helper `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` below. This
1197	isolates the entire substrate-gap content of the inductive step into a
1198	single named typed-`sorry` declaration with a precise iter-196+
1199	re-engagement plan, rather than an opaque inline `sorry` at the case-split
1200	site. The main theorem then dispatches the `n > 0` branch by a one-line
1201	`exact` call to the helper.
1202	
1203	Per the iter-195 Lane G directive (OFF-CRITICAL-PATH minimal dispatch),
1204	this is a **structural commit** (option (b): carving with iter-196 re-
1205	engagement timeline). The HARD BAR is met; closure is deferred. -/
1206	
1207	/-- **Lane G iter-195 structural carving (typed-`sorry` substrate-gap helper).**
1208	
1209	The `pd_R(M) = k + 1` inductive step of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula.
1210	
1211	**Blueprint proof shape** (`sec:ab_main` inductive step, Stacks 090V).
1212	Strong induction on `depth(M)`:
1213	
1214	* **Sub-case `depth(M) = 0`.** Let `e := pd_R(M) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1215	  `lemma-add-trivial-complex` (minimal-finite-free-resolution carving),
1216	  `M` admits a minimal resolution
1217	  `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → ⋯ → R^{n_0} → M → 0` with all matrix
1218	  coefficients in `𝔪`. The "what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF)
1219	  gives `depth(R) ≥ e`. Iterating `depth_of_short_exact` on the
1220	  short-exact splits `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → K_{e-2} → 0`, …,
1221	  `0 → K_0 → R^{n_0} → M → 0` gives
1222	  `depth(K_{e-c}) ≥ depth(R) - (c - 1)` and finally
1223	  `depth(M) ≥ depth(R) - e`. Since `depth(M) = 0`, also `depth(R) ≤ e`,
1224	  hence `depth(R) = e = (k + 1) + depth(M)`.
1225	
1226	* **Sub-case `depth(M) > 0`.** Pick `x ∈ 𝔪` a common NZD on both `R`
1227	  and `M` (Stacks `lemma-depth-sum-direct-sum` ⟹ `depth(R ⊕ M) > 0`,
1228	  hence a common NZD via prime avoidance over `Ass(R) ∪ Ass(M)`). The
1229	  snake lemma applied to multiplication by `x` on a minimal resolution
1230	  of `M` produces a minimal resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the
1231	  same length, so `pd_{R/xR}(M/xM) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1232	  `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`, `depth(R/xR) = depth(R) - 1` and
1233	  `depth(M/xM) = depth(M) - 1`, with regular sequences in `𝔪`
1234	  corresponding to those in `𝔪/(x)`, so
1235	  `depth_R(M/xM) = depth_{R/xR}(M/xM)`. The IH on `M/xM` over `R/xR`
1236	  gives `(k + 1) + (depth(M) - 1) = depth(R) - 1`, i.e.
1237	  `(k + 1) + depth(M) = depth(R)`.
1238	
1239	**Mathlib substrate gaps** (iter-196+ targets; pinned-commit `b80f227`
1240	audit per chapter `Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex` L554-560). All four
1241	pieces are absent:
1242	
1243	1. **Minimal finite free resolutions** (Stacks `lemma-add-trivial-complex`):
1244	   trim any finite free resolution to one with entries in `𝔪 P_i`.
1245	   ~80-120 LOC, independent and testable in isolation.
1246	2. **"What is exact" criterion** (Stacks 00MF, `proposition-what-exact`):
1247	   exactness ↔ depth-of-`r`-minor-ideals bound. ~150-200 LOC.
1248	3. **Snake-lemma-on-resolution**: tensoring a minimal resolution by
1249	   `R/xR` preserves minimal-exact (uses `x` NZD on each syzygy module).
1250	   ~80-120 LOC; depends on (1).
1251	4. **Depth-drops-by-one** (Stacks `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`): for `x`
1252	   an `M`-NZD, `depth(M/xM) = depth(M) - 1`. ~40-60 LOC; routes
1253	   through the existing `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` infrastructure
1254	   plus the SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0` LES of `Ext^*(κ, -)`.
1255	
1256	**iter-196+ re-engagement plan.** Estimated 4-8 iters total for full
1257	substrate work (matching the chapter L554-560 NOTE).
1258	
1259	* **iter-196 first slice:** piece (4) `depth-drops-by-one` — smallest;
1260	  the LES infrastructure (`covariant_sequence_exact₁`, …,
1261	  `covariant_sequence_exact₃`) and the `IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact`
1262	  packaging are already in this file (used inside
1263	  `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` and `depth_of_short_exact`).
1264	* **iter-197:** piece (1) minimal-resolution carving — independent;
1265	  reduces to the well-known "remove a trivial summand if one transition
1266	  is split" induction.
1267	* **iter-198-199:** piece (3) snake-lemma-on-resolution — depends on (1).
1268	* **iter-200+:** piece (2) "what is exact" criterion — the largest gap;
1269	  candidate for Mathlib upstream PR rather than project-side build.
1270	
1271	After all four pieces land, this helper's body becomes ~50-80 LOC of
1272	assembly following the blueprint proof shape above.
1273	
1274	**Why OFF-CRITICAL-PATH.** Per the iter-194 review and chapter
1275	L554-560 NOTE, the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula itself is NOT the gating
1276	consumer for A.4.a (the surface-extension proof). The gating consumer
1277	is `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` (Corollary `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`,
1278	this file §7), whose substrate gap is the single Stacks 00NQ
1279	implication structurally narrowed in iter-189-194. The AB formula is
1280	needed for the broader `dim ≤ depth` discourse but is not on the
1281	critical path for the Albanese argument; this carving documents the
1282	substrate cost for resumption when the critical path frees up. -/
1283	private lemma auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd
1284	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1285	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1286	    [Nontrivial M] (k : ℕ)
1287	    (_hpd : _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R M
1288	        = ((k + 1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)) :
1289	    ((k + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) + Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1290	      = Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R := by
1291	  -- iter-195 Lane G structural carving. Body deferred per the four substrate
1292	  -- gaps documented in the docstring above. iter-198 Lane AB substrate progress:
1293	  --   (1) minimal-resolution carving (Stacks lemma-add-trivial-complex) — GAP
1294	  --   (2) "what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) — GAP
1295	  --   (3) snake-lemma-on-minimal-resolution (preserves pd under tensoring by R/(x)) — GAP
1296	  --   (4) depth-drops-by-one (Stacks lemma-depth-drops-by-one) — CLOSED iter-198:
1297	  --       `depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular` (above this lemma,
1298	  --       inside the `Module` namespace). Companion
1299	  --       `exists_isSMulRegular_of_one_le_depth` lifts a positive-depth hypothesis
1300	  --       to an explicit M-NZD witness in 𝔪.
1301	  -- Remaining iter-199+ targets: (1)→(3). The body becomes ~80-120 LOC of
1302	  -- assembly after the three remaining pieces land:
1303	  --   * Strong induction on `depth M`; the IH applies at depth M - 1 (smaller).
1304	  --   * Sub-case `depth M = 0`: needs (1)+(2). depth_R(M) ≥ k+1 from (2) (lower bound)
1305	  --     and depth_R(M) ≤ k+1 from iterated depth_of_short_exact on (1)'s minimal
1306	  --     resolution (upper bound).
1307	  --   * Sub-case `depth M > 0`: pick a common NZD `x` on R and M, via
1308	  --     `exists_isSMulRegular_of_one_le_depth` for M plus the "what is exact"
1309	  --     criterion (2) giving `depth R > 0`. Apply (3) to get
1310	  --     `pd_{R/xR}(M/xM) = pd_R(M) = k+1`; apply (4) on both R and M to drop
1311	  --     both depths by 1. IH on (R/xR, M/xM) at depth M - 1 closes the formula.
1312	  sorry
1313	
1314	/-- **The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula.** Let `(R, 𝔪)` be a Noetherian local
1315	ring and let `M` be a nonzero finite `R`-module of finite projective
1316	dimension. Then
1317	```
1318	  pd_R(M) + depth_R(M) = depth(R).
1319	```
1320	
1321	The hypothesis "finite projective dimension" is encoded by an explicit
1322	upper bound `n : ℕ` on the projective dimension (so the formula compares
1323	finite numeric quantities cleanly without `WithBot ℕ∞`-arithmetic
1324	subtleties).
1325	
1326	iter-195 Lane G: the inductive step `pd_R(M) = k + 1` is delegated to
1327	the structural-carving helper `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`,
1328	which packages the entire substrate gap (4 named Mathlib-absent pieces)
1329	into a single typed `sorry` with a concrete iter-196+ re-engagement
1330	plan. The base case `pd_R(M) = 0` closed kernel-clean iter-194 via the
1331	finite-free-module + `depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty` route. -/
1332	theorem auslander_buchsbaum_formula
1333	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1334	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1335	    [Nontrivial M]
1336	    (n : ℕ)
1337	    (_hpd : _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R M = (n : WithBot ℕ∞)) :
1338	    (n : ℕ∞) + Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1339	      = Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R := by
1340	  -- iter-193 Lane G: structural scaffold for Stacks 090V. We split on `n` to
1341	  -- isolate the base case `pd_R(M) = 0` (where `M` is finite free over a
1342	  -- Noetherian local ring) from the inductive step `pd_R(M) = k + 1` (which
1343	  -- inducts on `depth(M)` via the snake-lemma-on-minimal-resolution recipe).
1344	  --
1345	  -- The `depth_eq_of_linearEquiv` helper above (axiom-clean) is the substrate
1346	  -- piece used in the `n = 0` case to identify `depth(M)` with `depth(R^k)`
1347	  -- for `M ≃ₗ[R] (Fin k → R)`; the residual `depth(R^k) = depth(R)` step is
1348	  -- the remaining substrate gap (a single direct-sum-of-modules depth fact).
1349	  rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with hn0 | hn_pos
1350	  · -- **Base case `n = 0`**: `pd_R(M) = 0` ⟹ `M` projective ⟹ (finite + local)
1351	    -- `M` free ⟹ `depth(M) = depth(R)`. The formula
1352	    -- `0 + depth(M) = depth(R)` collapses to `depth(M) = depth(R)`.
1353	    subst hn0
1354	    rw [Nat.cast_zero, zero_add]
1355	    -- Step 1: unfold `_root_.Module.projectiveDimension` to the categorical
1356	    -- form, then apply `projectiveDimension_eq_zero_iff`.
1357	    have hpd' :
1358	        CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M) = (0 : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
1359	      unfold _root_.Module.projectiveDimension at _hpd
1360	      exact _hpd
1361	    obtain ⟨hproj, _hNonZero⟩ :=
1362	      (CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_eq_zero_iff _).mp hpd'
1363	    -- Step 2: `CategoryTheory.Projective (ModuleCat.of R M)` ⟹ `Module.Projective R M`.
1364	    have hMproj : _root_.Module.Projective R M :=
1365	      (IsProjective.iff_projective M).mpr hproj
1366	    -- Step 3: `Module.Projective` ⟹ `Module.Flat`.
1367	    haveI : _root_.Module.Flat R M := _root_.Module.Flat.of_projective
1368	    -- Step 4: `Module.Flat` + `IsLocalRing` + `Module.Finite` ⟹ `Module.Free`.
1369	    haveI : _root_.Module.Free R M := _root_.Module.free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing
1370	    -- Step 5: with `M` finite free + `Nontrivial`, identify
1371	    -- `depth(M) = depth(R)` via the `Module.finBasis` equivalence and the
1372	    -- axiom-clean `depth_eq_of_linearEquiv` helper. The residual is the
1373	    -- single, narrower statement: `depth(Fin k → R) = depth(R)` for `k ≥ 1`.
1374	    have hk : 0 < _root_.Module.finrank R M :=
1375	      (_root_.Module.finrank_pos_iff_of_free R M).mpr inferInstance
1376	    set k : ℕ := _root_.Module.finrank R M with hk_def
1377	    -- Build the equivalence `M ≃ₗ[R] (Fin k → R)` via the chosen basis.
1378	    let e : M ≃ₗ[R] (Fin k → R) := (_root_.Module.finBasis R M).equivFun
1379	    -- Transport `depth(M) = depth(Fin k → R)` using the axiom-clean helper.

7	    (lines 1023-1124). Stacks `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`: for a Noetherian
8	    local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, and `x ∈ 𝔪` an
9	    `M`-regular element, the depth of `M/xM` is exactly one less than the
10	    depth of `M`: `depth(M/xM) + 1 = depth(M)`. Proof routes through
11	    `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` on both sides plus the LES of
12	    `Ext^*(κ, -)` on `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0`, using that `[x]_*` is zero
13	    on every `Ext^*(κ, M)` (since `x ∈ Ann κ`) to break the LES into
14	    short-exact pieces. The two-direction bridge via
15	    `ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le` converts the natural-number indexed
16	    Ext characterization to the `ℕ∞` depth equality.
17	  - `RingTheory.Module.exists_isSMulRegular_of_one_le_depth` (lines
18	    1138-1166). Companion lemma: `1 ≤ depth_𝔪(M)` implies the existence of
19	    an `M`-regular `x ∈ 𝔪`. Proof unfolds `depth` to the regular-sequence
20	    supremum (Nakayama rules out the trivial-quotient branch under
21	    `Nontrivial M`) and reads off the first element of a length-≥1 witness.

29	## `depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular`
30	
31	- **Approach:** Convert both depths to Ext characterizations via
32	  `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`. The LES of `Ext^*(κ, -)` on the SES
33	  `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0` plus the fact that `[x]_*` is zero on
34	  `Ext^*(κ, M)` (via the existing `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator`
35	  helper + `x ∈ Ann κ`) gives a short-exact decomposition
36	  `0 → Ext^i(κ, M) → Ext^i(κ, M/xM) → Ext^{i+1}(κ, M) → 0` for each `i`.
37	  This translates to the equivalence
38	  `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M ↔ (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth(M/xM)`. The `ℕ∞` equality is
39	  recovered via `le_antisymm` using `ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le` on
40	  each direction.

100	-/
101	
102	set_option autoImplicit false
103	
104	universe u v
105	
106	open CategoryTheory
107	
108	namespace RingTheory
109	
110	namespace Module
111	
112	/-! ## §1. Depth of a finite module
113	
114	The `I`-depth of a finite `R`-module `M` is the supremum in
115	`{0, 1, 2, …, ∞}` of the lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in `I`
116	(provided `IM ≠ M`; if `IM = M` we set `depth_I(M) = ∞`). Mathlib `b80f227`
117	exposes the regular-sequence predicate `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`
118	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.RegularSequence`) but not the resulting
119	numeric depth function — that is the gap this declaration fills.
120	
121	Blueprint reference: `def:depth` (Stacks tag 00LF). -/
122	
123	/-- The **`I`-depth** of a finite `R`-module `M`: the supremum (in `ℕ∞`) of
124	lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in the ideal `I`.
125	
126	When `IM = M` (the "trivial-quotient" case, e.g. `M = 0` or `I = R`) the
127	supremum is taken to be `⊤` by convention. When `(R, 𝔪)` is local one usually
128	calls `depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M` simply *the depth* of `M`.
129	
130	iter-176+: the body is the supremum
131	```
132	sSup { (n : ℕ∞) | ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = n ∧ (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ I) ∧
133	                  RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs }
134	```
135	folded with the `IM = M` clause. The signature is non-tautological: it
136	asserts a function `(Ideal R) → (M : Type v) → ℕ∞` matching the Stacks
137	00LF definition.
138	
139	**iter-179 Mathlib-gap check (Lane F Target 2 STRETCH)**: a pinned-commit
140	audit of `Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.Depth` (the only file under
141	`Mathlib/RingTheory/*` containing the word "depth") confirms that Mathlib
142	`b80f227` ships only depth-zero lemmas (`IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff`)
143	and *not* the numeric depth function itself. The one-liner re-export route
144	is therefore not available; the body stays a typed `sorry` until an
145	iter-180+ body lane fills the supremum-with-`IM=M` clause directly. -/
146	noncomputable def depth {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (_I : Ideal R)
147	    (_M : Type v) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : ℕ∞ :=
148	  open Classical in
149	  if _I • (⊤ : Submodule R _M) = ⊤ then (⊤ : ℕ∞)
150	  else sSup { n : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = n ∧
151	    (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ _I) ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular _M rs }
152	
153	end Module
154	
155	end RingTheory
156	
157	/-! ## §2. Projective dimension
158	
159	Mathlib `b80f227` exposes the categorical
160	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension : C → WithBot ℕ∞` on an abelian category
161	with enough projectives (file `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.Projective.Dimension`).
162	For `R`-modules this specialises to `ModuleCat.of R M`. The blueprint pins the
163	`Module.projectiveDimension` name as the re-export that downstream consumers
164	can use directly on an `R`-module without first packaging it in `ModuleCat`.
165	
166	Blueprint reference: `def:projective_dimension`. -/
167	
168	namespace Module
169	
170	/-- The **projective dimension** of an `R`-module `M`, defined as the
171	categorical projective dimension of `ModuleCat.of R M`.
172	
173	This is a re-export of `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` specialised to
174	the abelian category `ModuleCat R`. The categorical definition is the
175	infimum (in `WithBot ℕ∞`) of `n : ℕ` such that all `Ext^i(M, -)` vanish for
176	`i > n`, equivalently the smallest length of a projective resolution of `M`.
177	
178	Mathlib has the categorical `projectiveDimension` and the module-specific
179	`ModuleCat.projectiveDimension_eq_of_linearEquiv`; the blueprint pins the
180	wrapper name so downstream consumers can write
181	`Module.projectiveDimension R M` rather than threading `ModuleCat.of`.
182	
183	The body is the one-line re-export
184	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)`; iter-178 closed
185	this declaration kernel-clean. -/
186	noncomputable def projectiveDimension (R : Type u) [Ring R]
187	    (_M : Type u) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : WithBot ℕ∞ :=
188	  CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)
189	
190	end Module
191	
192	namespace RingTheory
193	
194	namespace Module
195	
196	/-! ## §3. Depth via Ext characterisation
197	
198	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` with residue field `κ = R/𝔪` and a
199	nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the depth of `M` equals the smallest index `i`
200	at which `Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero (Stacks tag 00LP).
201	
202	This is the lemma A.4.a's downstream consumer ultimately reads off:
203	combined with the regular-sequence definition (`depth`), the Ext
204	characterisation provides both the *lower bound* (regular sequences exhibit
205	`Ext^i = 0` for `i < length(rs)`) and the *upper bound* (failure of any
206	extension lifts a non-zero element in `Ext^{depth}(κ, M)`).
207	
208	The signature pins the equivalence via the depth-bound `↔` `Ext`-vanishing-
209	below: `n ≤ depth(M) ↔ Ext^i(κ, M) = 0 for all i < n`. This is logically
210	equivalent to "depth(M) = smallest i with Ext^i ≠ 0" and is the form most
211	convenient for inductive proofs (Stacks 00LP proof: pick `x ∈ 𝔪`
212	non-zero-divisor, use long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` on `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0`).
213	
214	Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_via_ext` (Stacks tag 00LP). -/
215	
216	/-! ### Helper C (iter-183 Lane G, axiom-clean): `Ann`-killing of Ext via R-linearity
217	
218	For any `R`-modules `N, M` and any `x : R` in the annihilator of `N`, the
219	R-action `x • e` on `e : Ext^i_R(N, M)` is zero.
220	
221	Proof sketch: `x • e = (mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N)).comp e (zero_add i)` (by R-linearity:
222	`mk₀_smul + smul_comp + mk₀_id_comp`). For `x ∈ Ann(N)` the morphism
223	`x • 𝟙_N : N ⟶ N` is the zero map, so `mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N) = mk₀ 0 = 0`
224	(`mk₀_zero`), and `0.comp e = 0` (`zero_comp`).
225	
226	iter-183 Lane G: closed kernel-clean. This is the precise statement of the
227	Stacks-00LP "`x ∈ 𝔪` annihilates `Ext^*(κ, -)`" trick, lifted to the more
228	general `x ∈ Ann(N)` form so it covers both `N = κ` and `N = R/(x_1,…,x_k)`. -/
229	private lemma ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator
230	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
231	    {N M : ModuleCat.{u} R} {i : ℕ} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} N M i)
232	    {x : R} (hx : x ∈ Module.annihilator R (N : Type u)) :
233	    x • e = 0 := by
234	  -- Step 1: x • 𝟙_N = 0 in ModuleCat (the underlying linear map sends m ↦ x • m,
235	  -- which is 0 since x ∈ Ann(N)).
236	  have hkill : (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N)) = (0 : N ⟶ N) := by
237	    apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
238	    apply LinearMap.ext
239	    intro n
240	    change x • n = 0
241	    exact Module.mem_annihilator.mp hx n
242	  -- Step 2: x • e = (mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N)).comp e (zero_add i) by R-linearity.
243	  have hreflect :
244	      (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N))).comp e (zero_add i)
245	        = x • e := by
246	    have hmk : (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N))
247	                : Abelian.Ext.{u} N N 0)
248	        = x • CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (𝟙 N) :=
249	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_smul (R := R) x (𝟙 N)
250	    rw [hmk, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_comp,
251	        CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp]
252	  -- Step 3: substitute hkill to collapse mk₀ … to mk₀ 0 = 0, then zero_comp.
253	  rw [← hreflect, hkill, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_zero,
254	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]
255	
256	/-- **Depth via Ext characterisation.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`
257	with residue field `κ = R/𝔪` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the
258	depth-bound `n ≤ depth(M)` is equivalent to the vanishing of `Ext^i_R(κ, M)`
259	for all `i < n`. Equivalently, `depth(M)` is the smallest `i` at which
260	`Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero.
261	
262	iter-176+: the body proceeds by induction on `n` via the long exact sequence
263	of `Ext^*(κ, -)` applied to `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0` for a non-zero-divisor
264	`x ∈ 𝔪`. The base case `n = 0` is `Hom(κ, M) ≠ 0 ↔ depth(M) = 0`, which is
265	the standard "the maximal ideal contains a zero-divisor on `M` iff
266	`𝔪 ∈ Ass(M)`" characterisation (Stacks 00LC).
267	
268	iter-183 Lane G structural progress:
269	* Generalised the inductive hypothesis over `M` (so `ih` is universally
270	  quantified in `M`, allowing recursive use on `M/xM`).
271	* Base case `n = 0` closed kernel-clean (both directions trivial).
272	* New axiom-clean helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` (lines
273	  229–254) discharges substantive piece (a) of the Stacks 00LP proof:
274	  *"`x ∈ Ann N` annihilates `Ext^*(N, ·)`"*, via
275	  `mk₀_smul + smul_comp + mk₀_id_comp + mk₀_zero + zero_comp`.
276	* The inductive step's **backward direction** has the regular-element
277	  extraction stage closed structurally: from the `Ext^0(κ, M) = 0`
278	  hypothesis we derive `Subsingleton (κ →ₗ[R] M)` (via `mk₀_eq_zero_iff +
279	  ModuleCat.hom_ext_iff`) and then invoke
280	  `IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff` + `Ideal.annihilator_quotient`
281	  to obtain `x ∈ 𝔪` with `IsSMulRegular M x`, with
282	  `nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal` providing the `Nontrivial
283	  (M/xM)` instance for the recursive IH application.
284	
285	Residual `sorry`s (2 named inline branches):
286	1. **Forward direction**  `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M → ∀ i ≤ n, Ext^i(κ, M) = 0` —
287	   needs: extract regular sequence of length n+1 from supremum (handle `⊤`
288	   via Nakayama); cons-decompose via `isRegular_cons_iff`; LES of Ext on
289	   `IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact`; recurse via `ih` on M/xM.
290	2. **Backward direction**, final assembly — after the regular-element
291	   extraction (already done), needs: LES chase to derive Ext-vanishing on
292	   M/xM at indices `j < n` (using the new helper to kill the
293	   multiplication-by-x maps); apply `ih (M := M/xM)` to obtain regular
294	   sequence `rs'`; cons `x :: rs'`; conclude via `le_sSup`. -/
295	theorem depth_eq_smallest_ext_index
296	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
297	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
298	    [Nontrivial M] (n : ℕ) :
299	    (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M ↔

900	    rw [eq_top_iff]
901	    intro m _
902	    obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›
903	    have hsingle_mem :
904	        (Pi.single j m : ι → M) ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) := by
905	      rw [h]; trivial
906	    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const] at hsingle_mem
907	    have := hsingle_mem j (Set.mem_univ j)
908	    rwa [Pi.single_eq_same] at this
909	  · intro h
910	    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const, eq_top_iff]
911	    intro f _ i _
912	    rw [h]
913	    trivial
914	
915	/-- `QuotSMulTop r (ι → M) ≃ₗ[R] ι → QuotSMulTop r M` for finite `ι`,
916	obtained by rewriting `r • ⊤ = Ideal.span {r} • ⊤` and using
917	`Submodule.quotientPi`. -/
918	private noncomputable def quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv
919	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (r : R)
920	    {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
921	    QuotSMulTop r (ι → M) ≃ₗ[R] (ι → QuotSMulTop r M) := by
922	  refine (Submodule.quotEquivOfEq _ _ ?_).trans (Submodule.quotientPi _)
923	  rw [← Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul r (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M))]
924	  rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const]
925	  congr 1
926	  funext _
927	  exact Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul r ⊤
928	
929	/-- For nonempty finite `ι`, a list `rs : List R` is `(ι → M)`-regular iff it
930	is `M`-regular. Proof by induction on `rs`: the empty case reduces to
931	`Nontrivial (ι → M) ↔ Nontrivial M`; the cons case uses `Pi.isSMulRegular_iff`
932	(for the SMul-regular conjunct) plus `quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv` +
933	`LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr` (to bridge the quotient regularity to the IH on
934	`QuotSMulTop r M`). -/
935	private lemma isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty
936	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nonempty ι]
937	    (rs : List R) :
938	    ∀ {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M],
939	      RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular (ι → M) rs ↔
940	        RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs := by
941	  induction rs with
942	  | nil =>
943	    intro M _ _
944	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
945	    · rintro ⟨_, hPi_top⟩
946	      refine ⟨.nil R M, ?_⟩
947	      rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, Submodule.bot_smul] at hPi_top ⊢
948	      intro habs
949	      apply hPi_top
950	      rw [Submodule.eq_bot_iff] at habs ⊢
951	      intro f _
952	      funext i
953	      exact habs (f i) trivial
954	    · rintro ⟨_, hM_top⟩
955	      refine ⟨.nil R (ι → M), ?_⟩
956	      rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, Submodule.bot_smul] at hM_top ⊢
957	      obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›
958	      intro habs
959	      apply hM_top
960	      rw [Submodule.eq_bot_iff] at habs ⊢
961	      intro m _
962	      have hsingle : (Pi.single j m : ι → M) = 0 := habs _ trivial
963	      have heval := congr_fun hsingle j
964	      rwa [Pi.single_eq_same, Pi.zero_apply] at heval
965	  | cons r rs' ih =>
966	    intro M _ _
967	    rw [RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff, RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff]
968	    refine and_congr ?_ ?_
969	    · constructor
970	      · intro h
971	        obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›
972	        exact Pi.isSMulRegular_iff.mp h j
973	      · intro h
974	        exact Pi.isSMulRegular_iff.mpr fun _ => h
975	    · rw [LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr
976	        (quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv (R := R) (ι := ι) r (M := M)) rs']
977	      exact ih (M := QuotSMulTop r M)

979	/-- **Main iter-194 Lane G closure.** For any commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`,
980	`R`-module `M`, and nonempty finite type `ι`, the depth of the Pi module
981	`ι → M` equals the depth of `M`:
982	```
983	  depth I (ι → M) = depth I M.
984	```
985	This is the substrate for the `pd_R(M) = 0` case of the Auslander–Buchsbaum
986	formula: a finite free module `M ≃ₗ[R] Fin k → R` has `depth(M) = depth(R)`,
987	so `0 + depth(M) = depth(R)` holds. -/
988	lemma depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty
989	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R)
990	    {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nonempty ι]
991	    {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
992	    depth I (ι → M) = depth I M := by
993	  unfold depth
994	  by_cases h : I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) = ⊤
995	  · rw [if_pos h, if_pos ((ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff I).mp h)]
996	  · rw [if_neg h, if_neg (mt (ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff I).mpr h)]
997	    congr 1
998	    ext n
999	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
1000	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
1001	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty rs).mp hreg⟩
1002	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
1003	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (isRegular_pi_const_iff_of_nonempty rs).mpr hreg⟩
1004	
1005	/-! ### Helper iter-198 Lane AB (axiom-clean): depth drops by exactly one for a regular
1006	element (Stacks `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`).
1007	
1008	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, and `x ∈ 𝔪` an
1009	`M`-regular element, the depth of `M/xM` is exactly one less than the depth of `M`:
1010	`depth(M/xM) + 1 = depth(M)`.
1011	
1012	This is one of the four substrate gaps for the Auslander–Buchsbaum n=k+1 step (the
1013	"depth-drops-by-one" piece). The proof packages the long-exact sequence of
1014	`Ext^*(κ, -)` on the SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0`, using that
1015	`[x]` is zero on every `Ext^*(κ, M)` (since `x ∈ Ann κ`) to break the LES into short
1016	exact pieces `0 → Ext^i(κ, M) → Ext^i(κ, M/xM) → Ext^{i+1}(κ, M) → 0`.
1017	
1018	Routed through `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` on both sides + the `ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le`
1019	two-direction bridge that converts a natural-number indexed predicate to a `ℕ∞` inequality. -/
1020	lemma depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular
1021	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1022	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1023	    [Nontrivial M] {x : R} (hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1024	    (hxReg : IsSMulRegular M x) :
1025	    depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) + 1 =
1026	      depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
1027	  haveI hMxM_nt : Nontrivial (QuotSMulTop x M) :=
1028	    nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal M hxMem
1029	  haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R (QuotSMulTop x M) := inferInstance
1030	  -- κ as a ModuleCat for compact notation.
1031	  set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) with hκ
1032	  -- x annihilates κ.
1033	  have hxAnnih : x ∈ _root_.Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) := by
1034	    have h : _root_.Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
1035	        = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := Ideal.annihilator_quotient
1036	    rw [h]; exact hxMem
1037	  -- The SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0`.
1038	  let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
1039	    ModuleCat.smulShortComplex (ModuleCat.of R M) x
1040	  have hS : S.ShortExact := hxReg.smulShortComplex_shortExact
1041	  have hSf_eq_smul : S.f = x • (𝟙 (ModuleCat.of R M) : _ ⟶ _) := rfl
1042	  -- `[x]_*` is zero on every `Ext^i(κ, M)` via the helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator`.
1043	  have hSf_kill : ∀ {i : ℕ} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ (ModuleCat.of R M) i),
1044	      e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = 0 := by
1045	    intro i e
1046	    have hcomp :
1047	        e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = x • e := by
1048	      rw [hSf_eq_smul]
1049	      exact (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_eq_comp_mk₀ e x).symm
1050	    rw [hcomp]
1051	    exact ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator e hxAnnih
1052	  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
1053	  · -- (≥): depth(M/xM) + 1 ≤ depth M.
1054	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
1055	    intro a ha
1056	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos a with h0 | hpos
1057	    · subst h0; exact bot_le
1058	    · obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hpos)
1059	      have hcast : ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (n : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
1060	      have hcast' : (Nat.succ n : ℕ) = n + 1 := rfl
1061	      rw [hcast'] at ha
1062	      rw [hcast] at ha
1063	      have hone_ne_top : (1 : ℕ∞) ≠ ⊤ := by simp
1064	      have hn_le_MxM :
1065	          (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) :=
1066	        (ENat.add_le_add_iff_right hone_ne_top).mp ha
1067	      have hMxM_vanish : ∀ j : ℕ, j < n → ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ
1068	            (ModuleCat.of R (QuotSMulTop x M)) j, e = 0 :=
1069	        (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := QuotSMulTop x M) n).mp hn_le_MxM
1070	      change ((n.succ : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1071	      rw [hcast']
1072	      rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
1073	      intro i hi e
1074	      rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos i with hi0 | hi_pos
1075	      · subst hi0
1076	        -- i = 0 case: x being M-regular ⟹ S.f mono ⟹ postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono.
1077	        haveI hmono : CategoryTheory.Mono S.f := by
1078	          rw [ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
1079	          exact hxReg
1080	        have hinj := CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono κ S.f
1081	        apply hinj
1082	        change e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero 0)
1083	          = (0 : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ (ModuleCat.of R M) 0).comp
1084	              (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero 0)
1085	        rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]
1086	        exact hSf_kill e
1087	      · -- i ≥ 1: write i = j + 1, use `covariant_sequence_exact₁`.
1088	        obtain ⟨j, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero
1089	          (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hi_pos)
1090	        have hj : j < n := by omega
1091	        obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
1092	          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ κ hS e (hSf_kill e) rfl
1093	        have hx₃_zero : x₃ = 0 := hMxM_vanish j hj _
1094	        rw [hx₃_zero] at hx₃
1095	        simpa using hx₃.symm
1096	  · -- (≤): depth M ≤ depth(M/xM) + 1.
1097	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
1098	    intro a ha
1099	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos a with h0 | hpos

1095	        simpa using hx₃.symm
1096	  · -- (≤): depth M ≤ depth(M/xM) + 1.
1097	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
1098	    intro a ha
1099	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos a with h0 | hpos
1100	    · subst h0; exact bot_le
1101	    · obtain ⟨m, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hpos)
1102	      have hcast' : (Nat.succ m : ℕ) = m + 1 := rfl
1103	      rw [hcast'] at ha
1104	      have hM_vanish : ∀ i : ℕ, i < m + 1 → ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ
1105	            (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 :=
1106	        (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) (m + 1)).mp ha
1107	      have hcast : ((m + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (m : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
1108	      change ((m.succ : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) + 1
1109	      rw [hcast', hcast]
1110	      -- It suffices to show m ≤ depth(M/xM).
1111	      have hMxM_bound :
1112	          (m : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (QuotSMulTop x M) := by
1113	        rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
1114	        intro j hj e
1115	        -- Use `covariant_sequence_exact₃` at n₀ = j, n₁ = j + 1.
1116	        have he_extClass : e.comp hS.extClass (rfl : j + 1 = j + 1) = 0 :=
1117	          hM_vanish (j + 1) (by omega) _
1118	        obtain ⟨x₂, hx₂⟩ :=
1119	          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ κ hS e rfl he_extClass
1120	        have hx₂_zero : x₂ = 0 := hM_vanish j (by omega) _
1121	        rw [hx₂_zero] at hx₂
1122	        simpa using hx₂.symm
1123	      gcongr
1124	
1125	/-! ### Helper iter-198 Lane AB (axiom-clean): regular-element existence from positive depth.
1126	
1127	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the assertion
1128	`1 ≤ depth_𝔪(M)` is equivalent to the existence of an `M`-regular element `x ∈ 𝔪`. This
1129	is a direct consequence of unfolding the depth definition to a regular-sequence supremum
1130	(Nakayama rules out the `𝔪 • ⊤ = ⊤` trivialisation in the `Nontrivial M` setting) and
1131	reading off a length-1 witness.
1132	
1133	This is the substrate companion to `depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular`: the
1134	two together implement the Stacks tag 00LE step "depth-drops-by-one for any regular
1135	element" plus its prerequisite "the regular element exists". -/
1136	lemma exists_isSMulRegular_of_one_le_depth
1137	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1138	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1139	    [Nontrivial M]
1140	    (h : (1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M) :
1141	    ∃ x : R, x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ∧ IsSMulRegular M x := by
1142	  -- Unfold depth: `𝔪 • ⊤ ≠ ⊤` by Nakayama in the `Nontrivial M` finite setting.
1143	  have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
1144	    Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
1145	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
1146	  have hdepth_unfold :
1147	      depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1148	        = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
1149	          (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1150	          ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
1151	    rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
1152	  rw [hdepth_unfold] at h
1153	  have hlt : (0 : ℕ∞) < sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
1154	      (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1155	      ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
1156	    calc (0 : ℕ∞) < 1 := by norm_num
1157	      _ ≤ _ := h
1158	  obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs, hrs_len, hrs_mem, hrs_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ := lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
1159	  -- rs has positive length (since (0 : ℕ∞) < ↑rs.length).
1160	  have hrs_pos : 0 < rs.length := by
1161	    by_contra h_neg
1162	    push Not at h_neg
1163	    have hk0 : rs.length = 0 := Nat.le_zero.mp h_neg
1164	    rw [hk0] at hrs_len
1165	    simp only [Nat.cast_zero] at hrs_len
1166	    rw [← hrs_len] at hk_lt
1167	    exact absurd hk_lt (lt_irrefl _)
1168	  rcases rs with _ | ⟨x, rs_tail⟩
1169	  · simp at hrs_pos
1170	  · refine ⟨x, hrs_mem x List.mem_cons_self, ?_⟩
1171	    exact ((RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_tail).mp hrs_reg).1
1172	
1173	end Module
1174	
1175	/-! ## §5. The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula
1176	
1177	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
1178	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
1179	```
1180	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
1181	```
1182	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
1183	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
1184	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
1185	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
1186	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
1187	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
1188	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
1189	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
1190	
1191	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/
1192	
1193	/-! ### Lane G iter-195 structural carving: inductive-step helper.
1194	
1195	The `pd_R(M) = k + 1` inductive step of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula is
1196	extracted into the helper `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` below. This
1197	isolates the entire substrate-gap content of the inductive step into a
1198	single named typed-`sorry` declaration with a precise iter-196+
1199	re-engagement plan, rather than an opaque inline `sorry` at the case-split
1200	site. The main theorem then dispatches the `n > 0` branch by a one-line
1201	`exact` call to the helper.
1202	
1203	Per the iter-195 Lane G directive (OFF-CRITICAL-PATH minimal dispatch),
1204	this is a **structural commit** (option (b): carving with iter-196 re-
1205	engagement timeline). The HARD BAR is met; closure is deferred. -/
1206	
1207	/-- **Lane G iter-195 structural carving (typed-`sorry` substrate-gap helper).**
1208	
1209	The `pd_R(M) = k + 1` inductive step of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula.
1210	
1211	**Blueprint proof shape** (`sec:ab_main` inductive step, Stacks 090V).
1212	Strong induction on `depth(M)`:
1213	
1214	* **Sub-case `depth(M) = 0`.** Let `e := pd_R(M) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1215	  `lemma-add-trivial-complex` (minimal-finite-free-resolution carving),
1216	  `M` admits a minimal resolution
1217	  `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → ⋯ → R^{n_0} → M → 0` with all matrix
1218	  coefficients in `𝔪`. The "what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF)
1219	  gives `depth(R) ≥ e`. Iterating `depth_of_short_exact` on the
1220	  short-exact splits `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → K_{e-2} → 0`, …,
1221	  `0 → K_0 → R^{n_0} → M → 0` gives
1222	  `depth(K_{e-c}) ≥ depth(R) - (c - 1)` and finally
1223	  `depth(M) ≥ depth(R) - e`. Since `depth(M) = 0`, also `depth(R) ≤ e`,
1224	  hence `depth(R) = e = (k + 1) + depth(M)`.
1225	
1226	* **Sub-case `depth(M) > 0`.** Pick `x ∈ 𝔪` a common NZD on both `R`
1227	  and `M` (Stacks `lemma-depth-sum-direct-sum` ⟹ `depth(R ⊕ M) > 0`,
1228	  hence a common NZD via prime avoidance over `Ass(R) ∪ Ass(M)`). The
1229	  snake lemma applied to multiplication by `x` on a minimal resolution
1230	  of `M` produces a minimal resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the
1231	  same length, so `pd_{R/xR}(M/xM) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1232	  `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`, `depth(R/xR) = depth(R) - 1` and
1233	  `depth(M/xM) = depth(M) - 1`, with regular sequences in `𝔪`
1234	  corresponding to those in `𝔪/(x)`, so
1235	  `depth_R(M/xM) = depth_{R/xR}(M/xM)`. The IH on `M/xM` over `R/xR`
1236	  gives `(k + 1) + (depth(M) - 1) = depth(R) - 1`, i.e.
1237	  `(k + 1) + depth(M) = depth(R)`.
1238	
1239	**Mathlib substrate gaps** (iter-196+ targets; pinned-commit `b80f227`
1240	audit per chapter `Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex` L554-560). All four
1241	pieces are absent:
1242	
1243	1. **Minimal finite free resolutions** (Stacks `lemma-add-trivial-complex`):
1244	   trim any finite free resolution to one with entries in `𝔪 P_i`.
1245	   ~80-120 LOC, independent and testable in isolation.
1246	2. **"What is exact" criterion** (Stacks 00MF, `proposition-what-exact`):
1247	   exactness ↔ depth-of-`r`-minor-ideals bound. ~150-200 LOC.
1248	3. **Snake-lemma-on-resolution**: tensoring a minimal resolution by
1249	   `R/xR` preserves minimal-exact (uses `x` NZD on each syzygy module).
1250	   ~80-120 LOC; depends on (1).
1251	4. **Depth-drops-by-one** (Stacks `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`): for `x`
1252	   an `M`-NZD, `depth(M/xM) = depth(M) - 1`. ~40-60 LOC; routes
1253	   through the existing `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` infrastructure
1254	   plus the SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0` LES of `Ext^*(κ, -)`.
1255	
1256	**iter-196+ re-engagement plan.** Estimated 4-8 iters total for full
1257	substrate work (matching the chapter L554-560 NOTE).
1258	
1259	* **iter-196 first slice:** piece (4) `depth-drops-by-one` — smallest;
1260	  the LES infrastructure (`covariant_sequence_exact₁`, …,
1261	  `covariant_sequence_exact₃`) and the `IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact`
1262	  packaging are already in this file (used inside
1263	  `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` and `depth_of_short_exact`).
1264	* **iter-197:** piece (1) minimal-resolution carving — independent;
1265	  reduces to the well-known "remove a trivial summand if one transition
1266	  is split" induction.
1267	* **iter-198-199:** piece (3) snake-lemma-on-resolution — depends on (1).
1268	* **iter-200+:** piece (2) "what is exact" criterion — the largest gap;
1269	  candidate for Mathlib upstream PR rather than project-side build.
1270	
1271	After all four pieces land, this helper's body becomes ~50-80 LOC of
1272	assembly following the blueprint proof shape above.
1273	
1274	**Why OFF-CRITICAL-PATH.** Per the iter-194 review and chapter
1275	L554-560 NOTE, the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula itself is NOT the gating
1276	consumer for A.4.a (the surface-extension proof). The gating consumer
1277	is `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` (Corollary `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`,
1278	this file §7), whose substrate gap is the single Stacks 00NQ
1279	implication structurally narrowed in iter-189-194. The AB formula is
1280	needed for the broader `dim ≤ depth` discourse but is not on the
1281	critical path for the Albanese argument; this carving documents the
1282	substrate cost for resumption when the critical path frees up. -/
1283	private lemma auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd
1284	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1285	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1286	    [Nontrivial M] (k : ℕ)
1287	    (_hpd : _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R M
1288	        = ((k + 1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)) :
1289	    ((k + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) + Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1290	      = Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R := by
1291	  -- iter-195 Lane G structural carving. Body deferred per the four substrate
1292	  -- gaps documented in the docstring above. iter-198 Lane AB substrate progress:
1293	  --   (1) minimal-resolution carving (Stacks lemma-add-trivial-complex) — GAP
1294	  --   (2) "what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) — GAP

input.code:
import Mathlib

universe u
open IsLocalRing TensorProduct

-- The goal: produce, for a finite module over a Noetherian local ring,
-- a surjection from a finite free module to M whose rank matches the
-- minimal number of generators (= dim of M ⊗ κ over κ).

example (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M] :
    ∃ (n : ℕ) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M), Function.Surjective f ∧
      n = Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (ResidueField R ⊗[R] M) := by
  set κ := ResidueField R
  set n := Module.finrank κ (κ ⊗[R] M) with hn
  -- κ ⊗ M is finite-dim κ-vector space; pick a basis (Fin n)-indexed.
  haveI : Module.Finite κ (κ ⊗[R] M) := inferInstance
  haveI : Module.Free κ (κ ⊗[R] M) := inferInstance
  let b : Module.Basis (Fin n) κ (κ ⊗[R] M) :=
    (Module.finBasis κ (κ ⊗[R] M))
  -- For each i, lift b i ∈ κ ⊗ M to m_i ∈ M.
  have hsurj : Function.Surjective ((TensorProduct.mk R κ M) 1) := by
    intro x
    induction x with
    | zero => exact ⟨0, by simp⟩
    | tmul k m =>
        obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective k
        refine ⟨r • m, ?_⟩
        rw [TensorProduct.mk_apply, TensorProduct.tmul_smul]
        congr 1
        simp [Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one, ← hr,
              show (Ideal.Quotient.mk (maximalIdeal R) r : κ) =
                (Ideal.Quotient.mk (maximalIdeal R)) r from rfl]
        sorry
    | add x y hx hy =>
        obtain ⟨a, rfl⟩ := hx; obtain ⟨b, rfl⟩ := hy
        exact ⟨a + b, by simp [map_add]⟩
  sorry

import Mathlib

universe u
open IsLocalRing TensorProduct

example (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M] :
    ∃ (n : ℕ) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M),
      Function.Surjective f ∧
      n = Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (ResidueField R ⊗[R] M) := by
  set κ := ResidueField R
  set n := Module.finrank κ (κ ⊗[R] M)
  -- Build a basis of κ ⊗ M.
  let b : Module.Basis (Fin n) κ (κ ⊗[R] M) :=
    (Module.finBasis κ (κ ⊗[R] M))
  -- Lift each basis vector via mk_surjective.
  have hsurj : Function.Surjective ((TensorProduct.mk R κ M) 1) := by
    apply TensorProduct.mk_surjective
    exact Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
  choose lift hlift using hsurj
  let m : Fin n → M := fun i => lift (b i)
  have hm : ∀ i, 1 ⊗ₜ[R] m i = b i := by
    intro i
    exact hlift (b i)
  -- The linear map f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M.
  let f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M := (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m
  refine ⟨n, f, ?_, rfl⟩
  -- Surjective: range of f equals span of m_i, which is ⊤ by Nakayama.
  rw [← LinearMap.range_eq_top, ← Submodule.eq_top_iff']
  intro x _
  have hspan : Submodule.span R (Set.range m) = ⊤ :=
    IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis m b hm
  have hxin : x ∈ Submodule.span R (Set.range m) := by rw [hspan]; trivial
  -- Express x as a sum and show it's in range of f.
  rw [Submodule.mem_span_range_iff_exists_fun] at hxin
  obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hxin
  refine ⟨c, ?_⟩
  simp only [f, Module.Basis.constr_apply, Pi.basisFun_repr]
  rw [← hc]
  rfl

import Mathlib

universe u
open IsLocalRing TensorProduct

example (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M] :
    ∃ (n : ℕ) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M),
      Function.Surjective f ∧
      n = Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (ResidueField R ⊗[R] M) := by
  set κ := ResidueField R
  set n := Module.finrank κ (κ ⊗[R] M)
  let b : Module.Basis (Fin n) κ (κ ⊗[R] M) :=
    (Module.finBasis κ (κ ⊗[R] M))
  have hsurj : Function.Surjective ((TensorProduct.mk R κ M) 1) := by
    apply TensorProduct.mk_surjective
    exact Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
  choose lift hlift using hsurj
  let m : Fin n → M := fun i => lift (b i)
  have hm : ∀ i, 1 ⊗ₜ[R] m i = b i := by
    intro i; exact hlift (b i)
  let f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M := (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m
  have hf_range : LinearMap.range f = Submodule.span R (Set.range m) :=
    Module.Basis.constr_range _ _
  have hspan : Submodule.span R (Set.range m) = ⊤ :=
    IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis m b hm
  refine ⟨n, f, ?_, rfl⟩
  exact LinearMap.range_eq_top.mp (by rw [hf_range, hspan])

863:private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const
894:private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff
906:    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const] at hsingle_mem
910:    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const, eq_top_iff]
924:  rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const]
995:  · rw [if_pos h, if_pos ((ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff I).mp h)]

855	identifies with `ι → M/r·⊤` via `Submodule.quotientPi`. The side condition
856	`I • ⊤ = ⊤` agrees on both sides via a `Pi.single` lifting argument.
857	
858	This is the substrate needed to close the `pd_R(M) = 0` case of the
859	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula (where `M ≃ₗ[R] Fin k → R` via a basis). -/
860	
861	/-- For any commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`, finite index `ι`, and module `M`,
862	the ideal-action `I • ⊤_{ι → M}` equals the pi-submodule of fibre `I • ⊤_M`s. -/
863	private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const
864	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι]
865	    (I : Ideal R) {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
866	    (I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M))) =
867	      Submodule.pi (Set.univ : Set ι) (fun (_ : ι) => I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)) := by
868	  apply le_antisymm
869	  · intro f hf i _
870	    refine Submodule.smul_induction_on hf ?_ ?_
871	    · intro a hain x _
872	      change a • x i ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)
873	      exact Submodule.smul_mem_smul hain trivial
874	    · intro x y hx hy
875	      change (x + y) i ∈ _
876	      exact Submodule.add_mem _ hx hy
877	  · intro f hf
878	    rw [show f = ∑ j, Pi.single j (f j) from (Finset.univ_sum_single f).symm]
879	    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
880	    intro j _
881	    have hfj : f j ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) := hf j (Set.mem_univ j)
882	    have hmap :
883	        Pi.single j (f j) ∈
884	          Submodule.map (LinearMap.single R (fun (_ : ι) => M) j)
885	            (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)) :=
886	      Submodule.mem_map.mpr ⟨f j, hfj, rfl⟩
887	    rw [Submodule.map_smul''] at hmap
888	    exact Submodule.smul_mono le_rfl le_top hmap
889	
890	/-- The side condition `I • ⊤ = ⊤` agrees on `ι → M` and `M` for nonempty
891	finite `ι`: a free product of fibre `I•⊤_M`-witnesses combines to a
892	`I•⊤_{ι → M}`-witness (via `Pi.single`-lifting), and conversely a
893	`Pi.single j m`-projection at `j` reads off the witness on the fibre. -/
894	private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff
895	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nonempty ι]
896	    (I : Ideal R) {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
897	    I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) = ⊤ ↔ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤ := by
898	  constructor
899	  · intro h
900	    rw [eq_top_iff]
901	    intro m _
902	    obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›
903	    have hsingle_mem :
904	        (Pi.single j m : ι → M) ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) := by

import Mathlib

universe u
open IsLocalRing TensorProduct

-- Try proving the kernel containment.
example (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M]
    (n : ℕ)
    (m : Fin n → M)
    (hb : LinearIndependent (ResidueField R)
      (fun i => (1 : ResidueField R) ⊗ₜ[R] m i :
        Fin n → ResidueField R ⊗[R] M))
    (x : Fin n → R)
    (hx : (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m x = 0) :
    ∀ i, x i ∈ maximalIdeal R := by
  -- Apply ResidueField R-base-change to the equation hx.
  -- 1 ⊗ Σ (x i • m i) = 0 in κ ⊗ M, but 1 ⊗ Σ (x i • m i) = Σ (π x_i) (1 ⊗ m_i).
  set κ := ResidueField R with hκ_def
  set π : R → κ := IsLocalRing.residue R with hπ_def
  have hx_eq : ∑ i, x i • m i = 0 := by
    have := hx
    simp only [Module.Basis.constr_apply, Pi.basisFun_repr, Finset.sum_apply] at this
    rw [Finsupp.sum] at this
    sorry  -- This will need work to reshape the sum.
  sorry

import Mathlib

universe u
open IsLocalRing TensorProduct

example (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
    (n : ℕ)
    (m : Fin n → M)
    (b : Module.Basis (Fin n) (ResidueField R) (ResidueField R ⊗[R] M))
    (hm : ∀ i, (1 : ResidueField R) ⊗ₜ[R] m i = b i)
    (x : Fin n → R)
    (hx : ∑ i, x i • m i = 0) :
    ∀ i, x i ∈ maximalIdeal R := by
  set κ := ResidueField R with hκ
  have h1 : (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (∑ i, x i • m i) = (0 : κ ⊗[R] M) := by
    rw [hx]; exact tmul_zero _ _
  rw [tmul_sum] at h1
  -- For each i, 1 ⊗ (x i • m i) = (residue (x i)) • (1 ⊗ m i) = (residue x i) • b i.
  have hrewrite : ∀ i,
      (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i)
        = (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i := by
    intro i
    rw [show ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i) : κ ⊗[R] M)
        = (x i) • ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i) from
      ((TensorProduct.tmul_smul (x i) (1 : κ) (m i)).symm)]
    rw [hm i]
    -- Now goal: x i • b i = residue R (x i) • b i (as κ-vector space)
    rfl
  rw [show (∑ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
      = ∑ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i from
    Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => hrewrite i)] at h1
  -- Linear independence of basis ⟹ each residue is 0.
  have hlin : LinearIndependent κ b := b.linearIndependent
  have hall : ∀ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) = 0 := by
    have := Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hlin (fun i => IsLocalRing.residue R (x i)) h1
    exact fun i => this i
  intro i
  have hxi : (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) = 0 := hall i
  rwa [IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff] at hxi

import Mathlib

universe u
open IsLocalRing TensorProduct

-- Full lemma: existence of a minimal surjection (with kernel in 𝔪 • ⊤).
lemma exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing
    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M] :
    ∃ (n : ℕ) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M),
      Function.Surjective f ∧
      n = Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (ResidueField R ⊗[R] M) ∧
      LinearMap.ker f ≤ (maximalIdeal R) • ⊤ := by
  set κ := ResidueField R with hκ
  set n := Module.finrank κ (κ ⊗[R] M) with hn
  let b : Module.Basis (Fin n) κ (κ ⊗[R] M) :=
    (Module.finBasis κ (κ ⊗[R] M))
  have hsurj_mk : Function.Surjective ((TensorProduct.mk R κ M) 1) := by
    apply TensorProduct.mk_surjective
    exact Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
  choose lift hlift using hsurj_mk
  let m : Fin n → M := fun i => lift (b i)
  have hm : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i = b i := fun i => hlift (b i)
  let f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M := (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m
  -- f sends x to Σ x_i m_i
  have hf_eval : ∀ x : Fin n → R, f x = ∑ i, x i • m i := by
    intro x
    rw [show f x = ((Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R) m x from rfl]
    rw [Module.Basis.constr_apply]
    have h : (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).repr x = Finsupp.equivFunOnFinite.symm x := by
      ext i; rw [Pi.basisFun_repr]; rfl
    rw [h, Finsupp.sum_fintype _ _ (by intros; simp)]
    exact Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => by simp)
  -- Range of f equals span of m.
  have hf_range : LinearMap.range f = Submodule.span R (Set.range m) :=
    Module.Basis.constr_range _ _
  have hspan : Submodule.span R (Set.range m) = ⊤ :=
    IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis m b hm
  refine ⟨n, f, ?_, rfl, ?_⟩
  · exact LinearMap.range_eq_top.mp (by rw [hf_range, hspan])
  -- Kernel inclusion.
  · intro x hx
    have hfx : f x = 0 := hx
    rw [hf_eval] at hfx
    -- Apply (1 : κ) ⊗ - to derive each residue (x i) = 0.
    have h1 : (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (∑ i, x i • m i) = (0 : κ ⊗[R] M) := by
      rw [hfx]; exact tmul_zero _ _
    rw [tmul_sum] at h1
    have hrewrite : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i)
        = (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i := by
      intro i
      rw [show ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
          = x i • ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i) from
        (TensorProduct.tmul_smul (R := R) (x i) (1 : κ) (m i))]
      rw [hm i]; rfl
    rw [show (∑ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
        = ∑ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i from
      Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => hrewrite i)] at h1
    have hlin : LinearIndependent κ b := b.linearIndependent
    have hall : ∀ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) = 0 := by
      have := Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hlin
        (fun i => IsLocalRing.residue R (x i)) h1
      exact fun i => this i
    -- Convert "each component in 𝔪" to "x ∈ 𝔪 • ⊤ in Fin n → R".
    -- Use the existing lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const.
    have hx_pi : x ∈ Submodule.pi (Set.univ : Set (Fin n))
        (fun (_ : Fin n) => (maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R R)) := by
      intro i _
      have : IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) = 0 := hall i
      rw [IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff] at this
      change x i ∈ (maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R R)
      rw [Submodule.smul_top_eq_comap_subtype_inv]
      sorry
    sorry

import Mathlib

universe u
open IsLocalRing TensorProduct

-- Concrete substrate: existence of minimal surjection R^n → M with kernel in 𝔪 R^n.
lemma exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing
    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M] :
    ∃ (n : ℕ) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M),
      Function.Surjective f ∧
      n = Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (ResidueField R ⊗[R] M) ∧
      LinearMap.ker f ≤ (maximalIdeal R) • ⊤ := by
  set κ := ResidueField R with hκ
  set n := Module.finrank κ (κ ⊗[R] M) with hn
  let b : Module.Basis (Fin n) κ (κ ⊗[R] M) :=
    (Module.finBasis κ (κ ⊗[R] M))
  have hsurj_mk : Function.Surjective ((TensorProduct.mk R κ M) 1) := by
    apply TensorProduct.mk_surjective; exact Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
  choose lift hlift using hsurj_mk
  let m : Fin n → M := fun i => lift (b i)
  have hm : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i = b i := fun i => hlift (b i)
  let f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M := (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m
  have hf_eval : ∀ x : Fin n → R, f x = ∑ i, x i • m i := by
    intro x
    rw [show f x = ((Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R) m x from rfl,
        Module.Basis.constr_apply]
    have h : (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).repr x = Finsupp.equivFunOnFinite.symm x := by
      ext i; rw [Pi.basisFun_repr]; rfl
    rw [h, Finsupp.sum_fintype _ _ (by intros; simp)]
    exact Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => by simp)
  have hf_range : LinearMap.range f = Submodule.span R (Set.range m) :=
    Module.Basis.constr_range _ _
  have hspan : Submodule.span R (Set.range m) = ⊤ :=
    IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis m b hm
  refine ⟨n, f, ?_, rfl, ?_⟩
  · exact LinearMap.range_eq_top.mp (by rw [hf_range, hspan])
  · -- Kernel inclusion via tensor-with-κ + linear independence of basis.
    intro x hx
    have hfx : f x = 0 := hx
    rw [hf_eval] at hfx
    have h1 : (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (∑ i, x i • m i) = (0 : κ ⊗[R] M) := by
      rw [hfx]; exact tmul_zero _ _
    rw [tmul_sum] at h1
    have hrewrite : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i)
        = (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i := by
      intro i
      rw [show ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
          = x i • ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i) from
        (TensorProduct.tmul_smul (R := R) (x i) (1 : κ) (m i))]
      rw [hm i]; rfl
    rw [show (∑ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
        = ∑ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i from
      Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => hrewrite i)] at h1
    have hlin : LinearIndependent κ b := b.linearIndependent
    have hall : ∀ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) = 0 := by
      have := Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hlin
        (fun i => IsLocalRing.residue R (x i)) h1
      exact fun i => this i
    -- Conclude x ∈ 𝔪 • ⊤_(Fin n → R).
    -- Use that 𝔪 • ⊤_(Fin n → R) = {y : Fin n → R | ∀ i, y i ∈ 𝔪}.
    have hx_pi : ∀ i, x i ∈ maximalIdeal R := by
      intro i
      have : IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) = 0 := hall i
      rwa [IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff] at this
    -- Now show x ∈ 𝔪 • ⊤. Strategy: x = Σ_i Pi.single i (x i), and
    -- each Pi.single i (x i) = (x i) • Pi.single i 1 ∈ 𝔪 • ⊤.
    rw [show x = ∑ i, Pi.single i (x i) from (Finset.univ_sum_single x).symm]
    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
    intro i _
    have : Pi.single i (x i) = x i • Pi.single i (1 : R) := by
      ext j; by_cases hij : i = j <;> simp [Pi.single, Function.update, hij]
    rw [this]
    exact Submodule.smul_mem_smul (hx_pi i) trivial

1175	/-! ## §5. The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula
1176	
1177	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
1178	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
1179	```
1180	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
1181	```
1182	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
1183	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
1184	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
1185	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
1186	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
1187	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
1188	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
1189	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
1190	
1191	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/
1192	
1193	/-! ### Lane G iter-195 structural carving: inductive-step helper.
1194	
1195	The `pd_R(M) = k + 1` inductive step of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula is
1196	extracted into the helper `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` below. This
1197	isolates the entire substrate-gap content of the inductive step into a
1198	single named typed-`sorry` declaration with a precise iter-196+
1199	re-engagement plan, rather than an opaque inline `sorry` at the case-split
1200	site. The main theorem then dispatches the `n > 0` branch by a one-line
1201	`exact` call to the helper.
1202	
1203	Per the iter-195 Lane G directive (OFF-CRITICAL-PATH minimal dispatch),
1204	this is a **structural commit** (option (b): carving with iter-196 re-
1205	engagement timeline). The HARD BAR is met; closure is deferred. -/
1206	
1207	/-- **Lane G iter-195 structural carving (typed-`sorry` substrate-gap helper).**
1208	
1209	The `pd_R(M) = k + 1` inductive step of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula.
1210	
1211	**Blueprint proof shape** (`sec:ab_main` inductive step, Stacks 090V).
1212	Strong induction on `depth(M)`:
1213	
1214	* **Sub-case `depth(M) = 0`.** Let `e := pd_R(M) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1215	  `lemma-add-trivial-complex` (minimal-finite-free-resolution carving),
1216	  `M` admits a minimal resolution
1217	  `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → ⋯ → R^{n_0} → M → 0` with all matrix
1218	  coefficients in `𝔪`. The "what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF)
1219	  gives `depth(R) ≥ e`. Iterating `depth_of_short_exact` on the
1220	  short-exact splits `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → K_{e-2} → 0`, …,
1221	  `0 → K_0 → R^{n_0} → M → 0` gives
1222	  `depth(K_{e-c}) ≥ depth(R) - (c - 1)` and finally
1223	  `depth(M) ≥ depth(R) - e`. Since `depth(M) = 0`, also `depth(R) ≤ e`,
1224	  hence `depth(R) = e = (k + 1) + depth(M)`.
1225	
1226	* **Sub-case `depth(M) > 0`.** Pick `x ∈ 𝔪` a common NZD on both `R`
1227	  and `M` (Stacks `lemma-depth-sum-direct-sum` ⟹ `depth(R ⊕ M) > 0`,
1228	  hence a common NZD via prime avoidance over `Ass(R) ∪ Ass(M)`). The
1229	  snake lemma applied to multiplication by `x` on a minimal resolution
1230	  of `M` produces a minimal resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the
1231	  same length, so `pd_{R/xR}(M/xM) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1232	  `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`, `depth(R/xR) = depth(R) - 1` and
1233	  `depth(M/xM) = depth(M) - 1`, with regular sequences in `𝔪`
1234	  corresponding to those in `𝔪/(x)`, so
1235	  `depth_R(M/xM) = depth_{R/xR}(M/xM)`. The IH on `M/xM` over `R/xR`
1236	  gives `(k + 1) + (depth(M) - 1) = depth(R) - 1`, i.e.
1237	  `(k + 1) + depth(M) = depth(R)`.
1238	
1239	**Mathlib substrate gaps** (iter-196+ targets; pinned-commit `b80f227`
1240	audit per chapter `Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex` L554-560). All four
1241	pieces are absent:
1242	
1243	1. **Minimal finite free resolutions** (Stacks `lemma-add-trivial-complex`):
1244	   trim any finite free resolution to one with entries in `𝔪 P_i`.
1245	   ~80-120 LOC, independent and testable in isolation.
1246	2. **"What is exact" criterion** (Stacks 00MF, `proposition-what-exact`):
1247	   exactness ↔ depth-of-`r`-minor-ideals bound. ~150-200 LOC.
1248	3. **Snake-lemma-on-resolution**: tensoring a minimal resolution by
1249	   `R/xR` preserves minimal-exact (uses `x` NZD on each syzygy module).
1250	   ~80-120 LOC; depends on (1).
1251	4. **Depth-drops-by-one** (Stacks `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`): for `x`
1252	   an `M`-NZD, `depth(M/xM) = depth(M) - 1`. ~40-60 LOC; routes
1253	   through the existing `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` infrastructure
1254	   plus the SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0` LES of `Ext^*(κ, -)`.
1255	
1256	**iter-196+ re-engagement plan.** Estimated 4-8 iters total for full
1257	substrate work (matching the chapter L554-560 NOTE).
1258	
1259	* **iter-196 first slice:** piece (4) `depth-drops-by-one` — smallest;
1260	  the LES infrastructure (`covariant_sequence_exact₁`, …,
1261	  `covariant_sequence_exact₃`) and the `IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact`
1262	  packaging are already in this file (used inside
1263	  `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` and `depth_of_short_exact`).
1264	* **iter-197:** piece (1) minimal-resolution carving — independent;
1265	  reduces to the well-known "remove a trivial summand if one transition
1266	  is split" induction.
1267	* **iter-198-199:** piece (3) snake-lemma-on-resolution — depends on (1).
1268	* **iter-200+:** piece (2) "what is exact" criterion — the largest gap;
1269	  candidate for Mathlib upstream PR rather than project-side build.
1270	
1271	After all four pieces land, this helper's body becomes ~50-80 LOC of
1272	assembly following the blueprint proof shape above.
1273	
1274	**Why OFF-CRITICAL-PATH.** Per the iter-194 review and chapter
1275	L554-560 NOTE, the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula itself is NOT the gating
1276	consumer for A.4.a (the surface-extension proof). The gating consumer
1277	is `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` (Corollary `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`,

/-! ### Helper iter-199 Lane AB-gap1 (axiom-clean): minimal surjection substrate.

For a finite `R`-module `M` over a local ring `R`, there exists a surjective
`R`-linear map `f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M` of the **minimal possible rank**
`n = dim_κ (κ ⊗_R M)` (where `κ = R/𝔪` is the residue field) whose **kernel
is contained in `𝔪 • ⊤`**. This is the first step of constructing a *minimal
finite free resolution*: iterating the construction on the kernel (which is
itself finitely generated when `R` is Noetherian) produces successive
syzygies whose differential maps each have image in `𝔪` times their target.

This is the first substrate piece of gap (1) (Stacks
`lemma-add-trivial-complex`) in the Auslander–Buchsbaum closure chain
(`auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`). It packages the basic
**Nakayama-lift** of a κ-basis of `κ ⊗_R M` to an `R`-spanning family in `M`
and reads off the kernel-containment from linear independence of the basis
combined with the `1 ⊗_R -` evaluation.

Mathlib substrate used (all axiom-clean):
* `IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis` — Nakayama lift of a κ-basis.
* `TensorProduct.mk_surjective` — the `1 ⊗_R -` map is surjective for
  `R/𝔪`-tensor.
* `Module.Basis.constr_range` — range of the linear extension equals span of
  the chosen image set.
* `Module.Basis.linearIndependent` — independence of a κ-basis.
* `IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff` — `r ∈ 𝔪 ↔ residue r = 0`. -/
lemma exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing
    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M] :
    ∃ (n : ℕ) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M),
      Function.Surjective f ∧
      n = _root_.Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
        (TensorProduct R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) M) ∧
      LinearMap.ker f ≤ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • ⊤ := by
  set κ := IsLocalRing.ResidueField R with hκ
  set n := _root_.Module.finrank κ (TensorProduct R κ M) with hn
  -- Pick a κ-basis of `κ ⊗_R M`.
  let b : _root_.Module.Basis (Fin n) κ (TensorProduct R κ M) :=
    _root_.Module.finBasis κ (TensorProduct R κ M)
  -- The canonical map `(1 : κ) ⊗_R -` is surjective.
  have hsurj_mk : Function.Surjective ((TensorProduct.mk R κ M) 1) := by
    apply TensorProduct.mk_surjective
    exact Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
  -- Lift each basis element to a representative in M.
  choose lift hlift using hsurj_mk
  let m : Fin n → M := fun i => lift (b i)
  have hm : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i = b i := fun i => hlift (b i)
  -- Define `f` by sending each standard basis vector of `Fin n → R` to `m i`.
  let f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M := (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m
  -- Evaluation: `f x = Σ x i • m i`.
  have hf_eval : ∀ x : Fin n → R, f x = ∑ i, x i • m i := by
    intro x
    rw [show f x = ((Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R) m x from rfl,
        _root_.Module.Basis.constr_apply]
    have h : (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).repr x = Finsupp.equivFunOnFinite.symm x := by
      ext i; rw [Pi.basisFun_repr]; rfl
    rw [h, Finsupp.sum_fintype _ _ (by intros; simp)]
    exact Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => by simp)
  -- Range = span of m.
  have hf_range : LinearMap.range f = Submodule.span R (Set.range m) :=
    _root_.Module.Basis.constr_range _ _
  -- Nakayama: span of `m i` equals all of `M`.
  have hspan : Submodule.span R (Set.range m) = ⊤ :=
    IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis m b hm
  refine ⟨n, f, ?_, rfl, ?_⟩
  · exact LinearMap.range_eq_top.mp (by rw [hf_range, hspan])
  · -- Kernel containment in `𝔪 • ⊤`.
    intro x hx
    have hfx : f x = 0 := hx
    rw [hf_eval] at hfx
    -- Apply `(1 : κ) ⊗_R -` to `Σ x i • m i = 0`.
    have h1 : (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (∑ i, x i • m i) = (0 : TensorProduct R κ M) := by
      rw [hfx]; exact TensorProduct.tmul_zero _ _
    rw [TensorProduct.tmul_sum] at h1
    -- Rewrite each summand: `1 ⊗_R (x i • m i) = residue(x i) • b i`.
    have hrewrite : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i)
        = (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i := by
      intro i
      rw [show ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
          = x i • ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i) from
        (TensorProduct.tmul_smul (R := R) (x i) (1 : κ) (m i))]
      rw [hm i]; rfl
    rw [show (∑ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
        = ∑ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i from
      Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => hrewrite i)] at h1
    -- Linear independence of `b` forces each `residue (x i) = 0`.
    have hlin : LinearIndependent κ b := b.linearIndependent
    have hall : ∀ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) = 0 := by
      have := Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hlin
        (fun i => IsLocalRing.residue R (x i)) h1
      exact fun i => this i
    -- Convert each component-in-𝔪 to `x ∈ 𝔪 • ⊤` via `Pi.single` decomposition.
    have hx_pi : ∀ i, x i ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
      intro i
      have : IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) = 0 := hall i
      rwa [IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff] at this
    rw [show x = ∑ i, Pi.single i (x i) from (Finset.univ_sum_single x).symm]
    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
    intro i _
    have hsingle :
        (Pi.single i (x i) : Fin n → R)
          = (x i) • (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R) := by
      ext j; by_cases hij : i = j <;> simp [Pi.single, Function.update, hij]
    rw [hsingle]
    exact Submodule.smul_mem_smul (hx_pi i) trivial

end Module

end RingTheory

        = ∑ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i from
      Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => hrewrite i)] at h1
    -- Linear independence of `b` forces each `residue (x i) = 0`.
    have hlin : LinearIndependent κ b := b.linearIndependent
    have hall : ∀ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) = 0 := by
      have := Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hlin
        (fun i => IsLocalRing.residue R (x i)) h1
      exact fun i => this i
    -- Convert each component-in-𝔪 to `x ∈ 𝔪 • ⊤` via `Pi.single` decomposition.
    have hx_pi : ∀ i, x i ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
      intro i
      have : IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) = 0 := hall i
      rwa [IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff] at this
    rw [show x = ∑ i, Pi.single i (x i) from (Finset.univ_sum_single x).symm]
    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
    intro i _
    have hsingle :
        (Pi.single i (x i) : Fin n → R)
          = (x i) • (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R) := by
      ext j; by_cases hij : i = j <;> simp [Pi.single, Function.update, hij]
    rw [hsingle]
    exact Submodule.smul_mem_smul (hx_pi i) trivial

end Module
